colors.name: earth-paper
colors.role: page background — the pale earthen render (흙벽) the whole atelier is drawn on; every ink is judged against it

colors.name: ink-beam
colors.role: all primary text and structural ink — the darkened beam wood; 10.5:1 on earth-paper, used wherever body text sits on a light ground

colors.name: wood-raw
colors.role: raw unfinished pine/hinoki — floor planes, maru deck, column fills; decorative surfaces only (3.1:1 on paper — never body text)

colors.name: beam-mid
colors.role: structural beams and column outlines; functional accent on the palest grounds (5.8:1 on earth-paper) and large display type

colors.name: earth-wall
colors.role: 흙벽 wall fills inside the axonometric and zone panels — the warm enclosed earth between the timbers; decorative

colors.name: courtyard-stone
colors.role: 마당 stone ground of the open courtyard zone; decorative surface (text never sits on it without a panel)

colors.name: eave-red
colors.role: 단청 trim accent — focus ring, ::selection, the curved-eave underline, the exploded-view key callout; 5.6:1 on earth-paper so it carries functional accents on light grounds; 3.0:1+ minimum everywhere it appears as large type

colors.name: ink-soft
colors.role: secondary text — beam-mid darkened to clear AA body (6.6:1 on earth-paper) for glosses, captions, the Korean sublines

colors.name: ink-faint
colors.role: tertiary captions and blueprint grid lines on earth-paper (3.4:1 — large/uppercase labels only, never body)

colors.name: sky-bone
colors.role: the open-sky/madang ground, cooler than the earth-paper; text panels lift off it (10:1 with ink-beam)

colors.name: snow
colors.role: winter overlay tint and the maru (cool hall) deck — never carries text alone

fonts.role: structural Korean serif — section titles, the parts of the building (처마/대들보/기둥/마루/주춧돌); the load-bearing voice
display Hangul serif — the hero name 한옥 and zone headings; the carved myungjo, heavier than the body
blueprint/joinery annotations — layer callouts, mortise-and-tenon dimensions, season labels; the draftsman's hand

texture: Two SVG registers, both feTurbulence. Page-wide: one static wood-grain sheet (fractalNoise, baseFrequency 0.012 × 0.48 for the long horizontal fiber direction of sawn pine, 3 octaves, alpha ~0.06) fixed over the viewport in multiply — the unfinished wood under everything, never animated. Inside the axonometric: an earthen-wall roughness layer (fractalNoise 0.9, 2 octaves, alpha 0.09, warm earth tone matrix) clipped to the wall fills, so the 흙벽 reads as troweled, not printed. Season shifts recolor only the sky-bone/madang ground and the eave-shadow angle — the wood-grain and wall textures hold still across seasons (wood and earth don't change; light does).

motion.name: ease-timber
motion.value: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) — structure settling: fast in, long rest, the way a joint finds its seat

motion.name: ease-eave
motion.value: cubic-bezier(0.65, 0, 0.35, 1) — the curved eave line: an even, loaded brush through the whole stroke

motion.name: explode-scrub
motion.value: smoothstep of scroll progress p∈[0.06, 0.78]; five layers separate axially — roof ±150, beams ±86, columns ±44, floor +96, foundation +168 (viewBox px) — outer/inner 3.4:1 travel ratio sells the Z-axis

motion.name: joinery-draw
motion.value: stroke-dashoffset 1→0 (pathLength=1); layer i draws over p∈[0.30+0.07i, 0.54+0.07i], labels fade at 1.5× the same window — the tenon is revealed as the joint opens

motion.name: char-split
motion.value: 800ms ease-timber per glyph; --d stagger 0.06s, aria-label on container + aria-hidden on animated spans; triggered on first in-view

motion.name: eave-path-text
motion.value: the hero subline rides the curved-eave path (textPath), offset animates 0 → resting over 1.6s ease-eave once; reduced motion parks it at rest

motion.name: season-swap
motion.value: 420ms ease-timber; the madang sky-bone ground and eave-shadow angle transition (transform/opacity/background-color of overlays only); wood grain and wall texture never move

motion.name: zone-reveal
motion.value: 0.7s ease-timber, translateY(22px) → 0 + opacity, 100ms stagger per observed batch — each climate zone rises into place as the beam it rests on

motion.name: eave-shadow-drift
motion.value: the courtyard eave shadow rect translates with season (spring +0, summer −6, autumn +8, winter +14 px) and recolors; transform only, capped
